reasoning: Based on polling data from late October and early November 2025, Pierre-Yves Bournazel (supported by the Renaissance party) is trailing significantly with about 14-15% of first-round voting intentions. He is far behind the main right-wing candidate, Rachida Dati, who commands 26-28% of the vote. In addition, the Left wing (Socialists, Greens, and LFI) has a combined potential of roughly 45% and historically dominates Paris mayoral elections under its specific district-based electoral system. Since Bournazel is deeply outpolled within his own broader political camp, he is highly unlikely to emerge as the leading candidate of the center/right in the second round. His path to victory would require a collapse of Dati's campaign along with an improbable victory over the strongly positioned Left in the second round. Therefore, his chances of becoming Mayor of Paris in March 2026 are exceptionally low.
